title: NBA Daily Rundown
subtitle: May 16, 2026: Playoff Thrills & Conference Finals Set!

items.headline: Conference Finals Locked: Spurs Face Thunder Out West, East Awaits Game 7 Winner!
items.summary: The NBA's Conference Finals picture is solidifying! The San Antonio Spurs have punched their ticket to face the reigning champion Oklahoma City Thunder in the Western Conference Finals. Meanwhile, the Eastern Conference awaits a decisive Game 7 between the Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers to determine who will challenge the New York Knicks for conference supremacy.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard a 'Dark Horse' in Trade Talks, Execs Say
items.summary: While Giannis Antetokounmpo has been a frequent name in trade rumors, anonymous NBA executives are reportedly putting Kawhi Leonard ahead of Giannis as a 'dark horse' in potential blockbuster trades this summer.

items.headline: Pistons Force Game 7 in East Semis!
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons delivered a dominant performance to defeat the Cleveland Cavaliers, forcing a crucial Game 7 in their Eastern Conference Semifinals series.

items.headline: Spurs Crush Wolves to Advance!
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs left no doubt, overwhelming the Minnesota Timberwolves in a blowout victory to clinch their spot in the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Stephon Castle Makes Playoff History!
items.summary: SAS rookie Stephon Castle exploded for a sensational 32 points, 11 rebounds, 6 assists, and 5 three-pointers against MIN, becoming the youngest player in NBA playoff history to record 30+ PTS, 10+ REB, 5+ AST, and 5+ 3PM in a single postseason game.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham Leads Pistons to Game 7!
items.summary: Cade Cunningham spearheaded the DET offense with 21 points, driving his team to a pivotal Game 7 victory over the Cavs.

items.headline: Fox & Wemby Dominate for Spurs!
items.summary: De'Aaron Fox chipped in 21 points and 9 assists, while Victor Wembanyama added a double-double with 19 points and 11 rebounds, powering the Spurs to the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Impactful Playoff Run
items.summary: While not in action last night, Israeli star Deni Avdija previously showcased his incredible talent with a 41-point, 12-assist, 7-rebound performance in a Play-In Tournament win and a 30-point, 10-rebound, 5-assist effort in the first round. His breakout season continues to impress!

items.headline: NBA MVP Finalists Announced: SGA, Wemby, Jokic Vie for Top Honor
items.summary: The league's most prestigious individual award is up for grabs! Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Victor Wembanyama, and Nikola Jokic have been named the finalists for the NBA MVP award, with the winner set to be revealed on Sunday.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Finals Matchup Looms Large
items.summary: With the New York Knicks already through, all eyes are on Sunday's Game 7 clash between the Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers to see who will earn the right to challenge the Knicks in the Eastern Conference Finals.
